heroHeadline: Amazon Kuiper Reaches 270 Satellites; FCC Set to Vote on Spectrum Rules That Could 7x Broadband Capacity
heroSubtitle: Amazon's April 27 record Atlas V launch pushed its LEO broadband constellation to 270 satellites, with mid-2026 commercial service confirmed — while the FCC's April 30 vote on satellite spectrum modernization could unlock 700% more satellite capacity, accelerating affordable internet access in underserved regions across Africa, South Asia, and the Americas
footerNote: Data sourced from ITU, GSMA, World Bank, A4AI, SpaceX regulatory filings, Amazon, FCC, and major international research outlets. Contested figures noted with confidence levels.
